articles.title: Fighting the Invisible Danger: Scientists Develop New Weapons Against Superbugs
articles.summary: Scientists from Hong Kong University have discovered a serious threat to our health: bacteria that can no longer be killed by regular medicines, called superbugs. These dangerous germs develop when antibiotics are overused and misused. Without action, experts warn that by 2050, these infections could cause more deaths than cancer. To tackle this problem, researchers have created a new skin cream for eczema that can control harmful bacteria without completely destroying them. This clever approach gives doctors a new tool to fight superbugs and saves antibiotics for when we truly need them. The breakthrough shows how scientists are working hard to keep medicines effective for future generations.

lv1: What are superbugs and why are they dangerous? [超級細菌是什麼，為什麼它們很危險？]
What did Hong Kong scientists invent to help fight superbugs? [香港科學家發明了什麼來幫助對抗超級細菌？]
lv2: How does overusing antibiotics create superbugs, and why is this a problem? [濫用抗生素如何導致超級細菌出現，為什麼這是個問題？]
How does the new eczema product work differently from regular antibiotics? [新的濕疹產品與常規抗生素的作用方式有何不同？]
lv3: If superbugs can no longer be killed by medicine, what might happen to society if the problem gets worse? How could we prevent this? [如果超級細菌無法被藥物殺死，如果問題惡化，社會會發生什麼？我們如何能預防這種情況？]

Some people believe we should stop using all antibiotics to prevent superbugs from developing. Others say this is unrealistic. What do you think is the right balance? [有些人認為我們應該停止使用所有抗生素以防止超級細菌發展。其他人認為這是不現實的。你認為正確的平衡是什麼？]

articles.title: Oil Prices Drop as Middle East Peace Talks Bring Hope to Asia
articles.summary: Crude oil prices have fallen sharply because of good news: talks between the United States and Iran suggest they might reopen a vital shipping route called the Strait of Hormuz. This narrow waterway is crucial for moving oil from the Middle East to Asia. The recent conflict had blocked this route, causing oil prices to soar. Now, as negotiations progress, traders expect the waterway will reopen soon, bringing prices down. However, experts warn that countries in Asia won't feel immediate relief. Ships still face backlogs, storage tanks are nearly empty, and some delivery routes have been damaged. Recovery will take time, even if the Strait of Hormuz reopens. Businesses and governments across Asia are watching closely to see if peace will stabilize their energy supplies.

lv1: What is the Strait of Hormuz and why is it important? [荷姆茲海峽是什麼，為什麼它很重要？]
Why did oil prices drop when the US and Iran started talking? [當美國和伊朗開始談判時，油價為什麼下跌？]
lv2: How did closing the Strait of Hormuz affect oil prices and countries that depend on oil? [關閉荷姆茲海峽如何影響油價和依賴石油的國家？]
Why might Asian countries not benefit immediately from reopening the Strait, even if the deal succeeds? [為什麼亞洲國家可能不會立即受益於荷姆茲海峽的重新開放，即使協議成功了？]
lv3: If a single narrow waterway controls the energy supply of an entire region, what risks does this create for countries? How could nations reduce this risk? [如果一條狹窄的水道控制著整個地區的能源供應，這對國家造成什麼風險？各國如何能減少這種風險？]

Some people argue that countries should reduce their dependence on oil to avoid conflicts over energy. Others say this is too expensive and unrealistic. What is your perspective? [有些人主張國家應該減少對石油的依賴，以避免能源衝突。其他人說這太昂貴且不現實。你的看法是什麼？]

articles.title: 港車北上政策延長五年 便利香港駕駛人士往返廣東
articles.summary: 廣東省政府宣布延長「港車北上」政策五年，有效期至2031年6月1日。這項政策讓香港車主可以不需加裝內地車牌，直接從港珠澳大橋進入廣東。駕駛人士每次可在廣東逗留最多30天，每年最多180天。據統計，已有超過14萬輛香港車輛登記參與此計劃，預計未來數年會增至20萬輛。香港汽車業界對政策延長表示歡迎，並期望政府進一步優化珠海口岸設施，改善邊界檢查效率，讓更多人受惠於粵港兩地的便利交通安排。

lv1: 港車北上政策是什麼？它允許香港車主做什麼？
這項政策延長至哪一年？
lv2: 為什麼政府決定延長港車北上政策五年？這對香港駕駛人士有什麼好處？
香港汽車業界對政策延長有什麼期望和建議？
lv3: 港珠澳大橋和港車北上政策如何促進香港與廣東的交流？這種跨境便利對兩地經濟有什麼影響？
如果更多香港人可以方便地前往廣東，這可能帶來什麼機遇和挑戰？政府應如何應對這些變化？

articles.title: 動物交通意外大幅增加 保護團體呼籲司機提高警覺
articles.summary: 過去五年，涉及動物的交通意外宗數大幅增加，去年共錄得346宗，較五年前增加逾八倍。這些意外通常發生在駕駛人士駕駛時未有留意路面情況。最近發生多起司機撞傷動物後逃逸的案件，引起動物保護團體關注。例如有隻幼犬遭車撞倒，更被司機疑似拖行，導致嚴重受傷。善心人士發現後將狗送醫，但傷勢太重最終不治。保護動物團體呼籲駕駛人士提高警覺，在行人路和偏遠地區減速行駛，同時提醒市民如發現受傷動物應立即報警或聯絡相關機構尋求幫助。

lv1: 動物交通意外在過去五年增加了多少？
當司機撞傷動物時應該如何做？
lv2: 為什麼涉及動物的交通意外數字會大幅增加？可能的原因是什麼？
司機逃逸後遺棄受傷動物會造成什麼後果？
lv3: 城市發展如何影響野生動物與人類的互動？我們應該如何平衡城市進展與動物保護？
除了提高駕駛人士的警覺，政府和社會還可以採取什麼措施來減少動物交通意外？
